page: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by custom 64-bit ARMv8 AWS Graviton2 processors utilizing the Neoverse-N1 microarchitecture. It features 48 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.5 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 192 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 2850 GB of NVMe SSD instance storage, and the instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and static web serving throughput both placing in the top 25%. Single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into average and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth is stable, registering average performance for both cached small-block reads and uncached large-block read/write operations. Cryptographic operations are supported via hardware-accelerated AES, though advanced vector and matrix extensions are absent due to the ARM64 architecture.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m6gd.12xlarge offers a high-density resource profile by combining dedicated ARM cores with substantial local NVMe storage, reducing the need for external block storage attachments. However, workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or complex vector instructions may experience bottlenecks, as evidenced by lower single-threaded compression and text processing benchmarks.

This instance is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, database operations, and caching layers like Redis. The combination of high-speed local NVMe storage and balanced memory makes it an effective choice for distributed data stores, software compilation, and medium-scale containerized microservices.
description: The Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 48-core AWS Graviton2 ARMv8 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 GB of RAM, 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Lacking hyperthreading, its 48 dedicated physical cores deliver strong multi-core performance, particularly in static web serving and multi-threaded CPU tasks, though single-core execution speeds are more moderate. The instance lacks AVX and AMX extensions but supports hardware AES cryptography. Qualitatively, the inclusion of high-capacity local NVMe storage alongside a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio offers a cost-efficient profile for resource-dense workloads. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, web serving, database operations, and caching layers.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is a general-purpose ARM64 instance featuring 48 dedicated Graviton2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for multi-threaded workloads and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6gd.12xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 48 dedicated cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2850 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A balanced ARM64 platform combining dedicated physical cores with high-capacity local NVMe storage for demanding multi-threaded and database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 48 dedicated AWS Graviton2 ARMv8 physical cores at 2.5 GHz.
Equipped with 192 GB of system memory and 2850 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high static web serving throughput.
Features a 20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
Best suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and caching layers.
